title: Tokens
description: Factory and work tokens: the unit of submitted work that occupies a work-type state as it moves through you-agent-factory.
openingSummary: A factory token, also called a work token, is the runtime unit that represents one submitted work item while it occupies a work-type state.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A factory token, also called a work token, is the runtime unit that represents one submitted work item occupying a work-type state. That state is called a place: the factory tracks where each token sits by the place it occupies. The token is the thing the runtime moves — not a chat message, and not an LLM tokenizer piece.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: When you read whether a workstation is enabled, inspect you work list or session work views, or ask why a submit lands work in an initial state, you are looking at factory tokens. Recognizing that unit of work makes those surfaces readable without reverse-engineering the rest of the topology first.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: You submit work for a task work type. The runtime creates a factory token in the initial place task:init. A workstation whose inputs include task:init becomes enabled, consumes that token, and dispatches its worker. When the worker finishes, the workstation routes the token to another place based on the outcome — an accepted success place, a continue place for ordinary partial progress, a rejection place, or a failure place.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Tokens appear on three observable surfaces. In factory.json, each work type and state pair becomes a place named like task:init. Workstation inputs and outputs wire those places so a step can consume a token and emit it on an outcome route. Inspection surfaces such as you work list and session work views show which tokens currently sit in which states.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Factory and work tokens are not LLM tokenizer or model-input tokens. In this system a token is the unit of submitted work that occupies a place; it is not a piece of text a model reads. Design-system or UI tokens are also a different idea and are out of scope here.
